page: Amazon Web Services c3.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2680 v2 processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The processor is equipped with a 25 MB L3 cache and supports AVX and AES instruction sets, though it lacks AVX-512 capabilities. The instance is provisioned with 15.0 GB of memory, yielding 1.88 GB per core, and includes 160 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.

Benchmark results indicate moderate multi-core capabilities alongside weaker single-core performance. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test and Passmark CPU score both rank in the average tier, while Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores fall into the poor and weak categories, respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance, with read-only cached blocks reaching 388.0 GB/sec and uncached large blocks reaching 50.08 GB/sec. Cryptographic and database operations, such as Passmark database tests, perform at an average level, whereas Redis SET operations and single-threaded gzip compression are weak.

Operating on the Xen hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps, the c3.2xlarge offers a balanced resource density for legacy compute workloads. Its qualitative cost efficiency is defined by its older hardware generation, which trades off raw single-thread speed for dedicated multi-core capacity and bundled local NVMe SSD storage. This profile makes it suitable for workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations, while it is less suited for modern LLM inference or highly single-thread-bound tasks.
description: The Amazon Web Services c3.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon E5-2680 v2 processor with 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and a 2.8 GHz clock speed. It is equipped with 15.0 GB of memory, 160 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 25 MB L3 cache. Operating on the Xen hypervisor, the instance provides a baseline network speed of 1 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core CPU capabilities, average memory bandwidth, and average software compilation speeds, though single-core performance is weak. This instance is suited for workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations, offering a legacy hardware profile with dedicated compute resources and bundled local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c3.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 15 GB of RAM, and 160 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, suitable for static web serving and moderate database workloads.

tagline: An x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring eight vCPUs, bundled local NVMe SSD storage, and balanced multi-core performance for legacy workloads.

15.0 GB of system memory and 160 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
